sanskrit: प्रयाणकाले मनसाचलेन भक्त्या युक्तो योगबलेन चैव। भ्रुवोर्मध्ये प्राणमावेश्य सम्यक् स तं परं पुरुषमुपैति दिव्यम्॥
transliteration: prayāṇa-kāle manasācalena bhaktyā yukto yoga-balena caiva | bhruvor madhye prāṇam āveśya samyak sa taṃ paraṃ puruṣam upaiti divyam ||
literal_meaning: At the moment of death / with an unwavering mind / endowed with devotion / and also with the power of yoga / between the two eyebrows / having fully placed the life-breath / he attains that Supreme Divine Person.
contextual_meaning: The verse prescribes a death-bed integration practice: stabilize attention at the Ājñā-cakra (eyebrow center) while flooding working memory with devotional representations of the Divine. This dual technique (yoga-bala + bhakti) overrides the default death-anxiety network, allowing the dying person to exit the body through the brahma-randhra with a coherent self-schema fused to the sacred object.
psychological_state: A rare, lucid death-bed state where terror is transmuted into concentrated love; the ego boundary softens into the devotional representation, producing a non-ordinary unity experience.
trigger_scenario: A hospice patient with terminal illness uses daily guided practice of eyebrow-center mindfulness plus loving-kindness imagery of their chosen spiritual figure; on the final night they report 'being held' rather than 'slipping away'.
anchor_text: Fix your mind on the Divine between your brows at death.
